headline: Malaysia Digital Nomad Visa Attracts 4,200 Remote Workers
context: DE Rantau digital nomad visa attracted 4,200 foreign remote workers to Malaysia in 2025 generating RM380 million in spending.

cards.big: DE Rantau visa attracted 4,200 digital nomads generating RM380 million
cards.sub: Programme targets high-income remote workers to boost local economies

cards.big: Average nomad earns RM15,000 monthly and spends RM8,500 locally
cards.sub: Penang and Langkawi most popular destinations with 62% of visa holders

cards.big: Programme created 1,200 local jobs in coworking hospitality and services
cards.sub: Average rental increases of 18% in nomad hotspots affect local residents

cards.big: 12 countries offer competing digital nomad visas in Asia-Pacific
cards.sub: Malaysia programme ranked 6th in Asia for attractiveness and value

cards.big: Digital nomad impact tests whether foreign worker programmes benefit or displace local communities
4,200 digital nomads on the new visa. Remote worker programmes succeed on ecosystem, not just tax incentives.
